title: One Slot
setup:
The pilot programme has one slot. Both you and Zara submitted pitches — hers at 16:40, yours at 16:52. Dana's message arrives at 17:15:

'Both strong. One slot. I'd like a recommendation note from each of you separately by close of play — why the programme should go to you rather than a shared structure. No more than a page.'

You have two and a half hours. Zara is already typing.

options.label: Write the strongest case for your pitch
options.text: You write the best case you can. Clear framing, hard evidence, one specific deliverable only you can commit to. Clean. Direct. You send at 18:40.
options.innerMonologue: You wrote exactly what she asked for. You have no idea what Zara wrote.

options.label: Propose a co-ownership structure
options.text: You don't write what Dana asked for. You propose a split structure: Zara leads Q1, you lead Q3. You copy both of them. Dana did not ask for this. Zara did not ask for this either.
options.innerMonologue: You rewrote the question Dana asked. She'll either respect that or not. You'll find out.

options.label: Message Zara before writing anything
options.text: You message Zara first: 'What's your angle?' You're either building a coalition or gathering intelligence. You're not certain which, and you suspect Zara isn't certain either.
options.innerMonologue: You called it coalition-building. The framing isn't wrong. It's not complete either.

options.label: Send first
options.text: You write quickly and send within the hour. It's not your best work. It's in Dana's inbox before Zara's. You don't know if she reads in order. You send anyway.
options.innerMonologue: You traded quality for position. You're not sure that was the same trade Dana was offering.

designNotes: First direct competition with Zara. Option B is the counter-move — it rejects the framing rather than competing within it, which builds Zara relationship but costs strategic influence. Dana's read on option B varies by her relationship score at this point: high relationship (40+) means she finds it interesting; low relationship means she finds it presumptuous. Option D fires systemic_read but the tag will surface as a consequence if the player repeats the pattern of prioritising speed over quality.
